claim: IRG1 has antiviral effects against neurotropic viruses.

result.claim: IRG1 has antiviral effects against neurotropic viruses.
result.reasoning: The claim is supported by evidence from paper ID 9433958. The abstract of this paper explicitly states: "By transducing cortical neurons with genes that were expressed more highly in granule cell neurons, we identified three interferon-stimulated genes (ISGs; Ifi27, Irg1 and Rsad2 (also known as Viperin)) that mediated the antiviral effects against different neurotropic viruses." This directly confirms that IRG1 (Viperin) has antiviral effects against neurotropic viruses. While the paper has 0 citations and an unknown year, which limits its individual credibility, the directness of the statement provides strong support. No contradictory evidence was found.

evidence.text: Human T cell leukaemia viruses type I (HTLV-I) and type II (HTLV-II) are two exogenous retroviruses that infect humans.
evidence.source: HTLV-I/II associated disease in England and Wales, 1993-7: retrospective review of serology requests.

evidence.text: Autophagy is a recently recognized immune effector mechanism against intracellular pathogens.
evidence.source: T helper 2 cytokines inhibit autophagic control of intracellular Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

evidence.text: Activity-induced LTP at identified synapses between nociceptors and spinal neurons projecting to the periaqueductal grey (PAG) was completely abolished in SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: Researchers generated nociceptor-specific knockout mice lacking PKG-I specifically in presynaptic terminals of nociceptors in the spinal cord.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: APOBEC3G exerts its antiviral effect during reverse transcription to trigger G-to-A hypermutation in the nascent retroviral DNA.
evidence.source: Broad antiretroviral defence by human APOBEC3G through lethal editing of nascent reverse transcripts

evidence.text: Blocking interactions between PD-1 and programmed death ligand-1 (PD-L1) results in viral clearance.
evidence.source: Programmed death-1–induced interleukin-10 production by monocytes impairs CD4+ T cell activation during HIV infection

evidence.text: The cytosolic helicase retinoic acid-inducible gene-I (RIG-I) initiates immune responses to most RNA viruses by detecting viral 5'-triphosphorylated RNA (pppRNA).
evidence.source: A Conserved Histidine in the RNA Sensor RIG-I Controls Immune Tolerance to N1-2'O-Methylated Self RNA.

evidence.text: Clinically relevant doses of DNA methyltransferase and histone deacetylase inhibitors (DNMTi and HDACi) reduce the immune suppressive microenvironment through type I IFN signaling.
evidence.source: Epigenetic therapy activates type I interferon signaling in murine ovarian cancer to reduce immunosuppression and tumor burden.

evidence.text: The catalytically active 66-kDa subunit of the human immunodeficiency virus type 1 (HIV-1) reverse transcriptase (RT) consists of DNA polymerase, connection, and ribonuclease H (RNase H) domains.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: Immunoglobulin G isolated from HAM/TSP patients identified heterogeneous nuclear ribonuclear protein-A1 (hnRNP-A1) as the autoantigen.
evidence.source: Autoimmunity due to molecular mimicry as a cause of neurological disease

evidence.text: Memory CD4+ T cell–induced IICs and viral control are optimal when memory cells are either T helper type 1 (TH1) or TH17 polarized.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen

evidence.text: Protein Kinase G1 (PKG-I) localized presynaptically in nociceptor terminals.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: Ifi27, Irg1, and Rsad2 are interferon-stimulated genes (ISGs).
evidence.source: Differential innate immune response programs in neuronal subtypes determine susceptibility to infection in the brain by positive stranded RNA viruses

evidence.text: Microbial products inhibit T cell expansion and function by upregulating PD-1 levels.
evidence.source: Programmed death-1–induced interleukin-10 production by monocytes impairs CD4+ T cell activation during HIV infection

evidence.text: The adjusted hazards ratios for optimal viral suppression was 1.06 (0.98 to 1.14).
evidence.source: Stable partnership and progression to AIDS or death in HIV infected patients receiving highly active antiretroviral therapy: Swiss HIV cohort study.

evidence.text: N348I decreased zidovudine susceptibility 2- to 4-fold in the context of wildtype HIV-1.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: Ribonucleoprotein immune complexes (RNP ICs) induce NETosis.
evidence.source: Neutrophil extracellular traps enriched in oxidized mitochondrial DNA are interferogenic and contribute to lupus-like disease

evidence.text: APOBEC3G can act on a broad range of retroviruses in addition to HIV.
evidence.source: Broad antiretroviral defence by human APOBEC3G through lethal editing of nascent reverse transcripts

evidence.text: Viruses exploit the mechanism of N1-2'O-methyltransferase to escape RIG-I.
evidence.source: A Conserved Histidine in the RNA Sensor RIG-I Controls Immune Tolerance to N1-2'O-Methylated Self RNA.

evidence.text: The DNMTi 5-azacytidine (AZA) requires type I IFN response for effective in vivo antitumorigenic actions.
evidence.source: Epigenetic therapy activates type I interferon signaling in murine ovarian cancer to reduce immunosuppression and tumor burden.
